steps.title: Identify the relationship
steps.content: Cause: 2023 measurements showed concerning rotation. Effect: officials closed the area to study stabilization. The blank links cause to effect.

steps.title: Pick the transition
steps.content:
The logical relationship is cause-effect: the tower's concerning rotation (cause) led officials to close the area (effect).
  • Choice A — "In comparison,": signals a similarity or contrast between two comparable things. No second thing is being compared here. ✗
  • Choice B — "Similarly,": signals that two things share a trait. No parallel case is introduced. ✗
  • Choice C — "As a result,": signals consequence — the closure is the direct result of the dangerous rotation. ✓
  • Choice D — "For example,": signals that a specific instance illustrates a general claim. The closure is not an example of a prior generalization. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
